title: usage of multiple column expressions in where-clause (f(a), f(b)) in (select a, b)  causes assertion failure on mserver5
poc: CREATE TABLE "dd_field" ( "TABEL" VARCHAR(25) NOT NULL, "VELD" VARCHAR(25) NOT NULL, "TYPE" VARCHAR(4) NOT NULL );
select "TABEL", "VELD" from dd_field f where ("TABEL", "VELD") in (select "TABEL", "VELD" from dd_field);
select "TABEL", "VELD" from dd_field f where (rtrim("TABEL"), rtrim("VELD")) in (select "TABEL", "VELD" from dd_field);
select "TABEL", "VELD" from dd_field f where (rtrim("TABEL")) in (select "TABEL" from dd_field);
key_bug_features: multiple column expressions in where-clause causes assertion failure
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE "test_table" ("id" INT PRIMARY KEY, "name" VARCHAR(50), "value" DECIMAL(10,2), "status" BOOLEAN);
SELECT "name", "value" FROM "test_table" t WHERE ("id", "name") IN (SELECT "id", "name" FROM "test_table");
SELECT "name", "value" FROM "test_table" t WHERE (COALESCE("name", 'default'), "value") IN (SELECT "name", "value" FROM "test_table");
